problem text: Consider any random variables $X$ and $Y$ with finite mean and variance $16$. Determine $\text{Corr}(X+Y, X-Y)$.
problem solution: Due to bilinearity, we find $\text{Cov}(X+Y, X-Y) = \text{Var}(X) - \text{Cov}(X,Y) + \text{Cov}(X,Y) - \text{Var}(Y)$. Given that $X$ and $Y$ share the same variance, all components cancel out, leaving the RHS as $0$, indicating $\text{Corr}(X+Y, X-Y) = 0$.
